item.title: 0.5.2 beta (build 40)
item.description:
Groovyday 0.5.2 private beta (build 40)

  • Fixed Ambient's visible periodic pop: its fine grain is now spatially stable rather than being re-seeded whenever the motion clock reaches an integer
  • Fixed Wave 2's disappearing subjects: its fleet is now a stable 30-object population (16 in compact/battery saver), with musical materials affecting their shared water rather than their existence
  • Lifted the complete Dockscape Waves horizon above the real Dock, so its distant water is visible above icons rather than hidden behind them
  • Double-clicking the visualizer no longer enters fullscreen. Dockscape height steps now animate smoothly at every transition: wing → 1.5× → 2× → max → wing
  • Right-wing scene controls now sit as one group beside fullscreen, and random uses a die rather than a music-shuffle icon
  • Every scene now preserves an intentional no-audio state: Wave 2's fleet gently drifts and heaves, Waves stay nearly flat but send a small periodic ripple through the surface, and Skyline windows slowly breathe with light
  • These idle motions are driven by the shared continuous motion phase and fade smoothly under real audio, so starting music feels like the existing scene waking up rather than switching modes
  • Particles now rescale their seeded field to the actual Dockscape width before the first frame, so they begin across the full scene instead of appearing center-born; their Dockscape material is also larger and more legible
  • Ambient now moves as a slower, directional wind field alongside Particles rather than a fast fly-through; Waves are lower-amplitude and more distant in both Dockscape and fullscreen
  • Skyline now takes a shared rhythmic lift from drums, Halo gains a supporting broken orbit, and Wave 2 varies its water-bound fleet from melody/detail so secondary subjects react without getting their own clocks
  • Dockscape is now the single windowed mode. The right wing has direct previous, random, next, and fullscreen controls; the left wing preserves artwork, title, artist, transport, and scrub information even in a narrow space
  • The entire Dockscape horizon is now a quiet resize target: hover anywhere along it to reveal a small local cue, drag to resize, or double-click to cycle Dock Wings → 1.5× → 2× → maximum → Dock Wings
  • The renderer is now organized around three reusable scene grammars — Vertical (Bars, Skyline, Halo), Wind (Ambient, Particles), and Wave (Waves, Wave 2)
  • Every scene declares a main musical actor plus secondary materials, so its primary motion reads first while supporting motion can enrich the scene without competing with it
  • Shared musical materials now include body, drums, beat, vocal, guitar, melody, and detail; vocal and guitar are intentionally stable spectral proxies for now, ready for a future audio classifier without rewriting scenes
  • The renderer now owns a smoothed display spectrum as well as the shared registers, so Bars, Skyline, and Halo use the same stable audio material instead of letting raw FFT frames reach their silhouettes
  • Master animation clocks are slew-limited, so no scene can tremble or speed-wobble on dense songs; Wave 2 contours and boats move as one broad body of water
  • Wave 2 rebuilt on the wave skeleton: beats become eased water energy, and strong onsets raise a single smooth swell with a cooldown instead of stacking
  • Waves and Wave 2 now build swells from shared bass envelopes and stored event energy; Wave 2 has a wider continuous sea response so boats and contours feel musical without vibration
  • New mode-aware stage mapping makes Dockscape a first-class presentation: the visible region above the real Dock is prioritized, while fullscreen and Dockscape share the same musical response grammar
  • Particles now fill Dockscape’s upper stage with larger, readable stars instead of clustering around the hidden center
  • Waves normalize their flow to screen width, so Dockscape no longer appears faster than fullscreen; every travelling breaker now enters from the left edge rather than popping into the middle
  • Wave 2 has a more energetic continuous water passage and lifts its fleet into Dockscape’s visible region; Ambient now has a coherent wind direction and music-driven gusts
  • Ambient gusts now travel continuously with no mid-scene reset; Particles begin across the full stage immediately in every mode and keep five persistent currents
  • Waves now share the same perspective geometry in fullscreen and Dockscape. Dockscape is slower only in time, while fullscreen’s smaller surface reads as more distant
  • Bars is unchanged in feel; its constants are the reference tuning for the new engine
  • Idle behavior is now identical everywhere: the same soft-wind baseline, settle timing, and instant wake on audio

Focus testing on first-frame composition and interaction: Particles should already occupy the full width in Dockscape; Ambient should read as slower wind, not a fast camera flight; Waves should feel more distant; and the full Dockscape horizon should resize or height-cycle without entering fullscreen.

item.title: 0.5.2 beta (build 39)
item.description:
Groovyday 0.5.2 private beta (build 39)

  • Every scene now preserves an intentional no-audio state: Wave 2's fleet gently drifts and heaves, Waves stay nearly flat but send a small periodic ripple through the surface, and Skyline windows slowly breathe with light
  • These idle motions are driven by the shared continuous motion phase and fade smoothly under real audio, so starting music feels like the existing scene waking up rather than switching modes
  • Particles now rescale their seeded field to the actual Dockscape width before the first frame, so they begin across the full scene instead of appearing center-born; their Dockscape material is also larger and more legible
  • Ambient now moves as a slower, directional wind field alongside Particles rather than a fast fly-through; Waves are lower-amplitude and more distant in both Dockscape and fullscreen
  • Skyline now takes a shared rhythmic lift from drums, Halo gains a supporting broken orbit, and Wave 2 varies its water-bound fleet from melody/detail so secondary subjects react without getting their own clocks
  • Dockscape is now the single windowed mode. The right wing has direct previous, random, next, and fullscreen controls; the left wing preserves artwork, title, artist, transport, and scrub information even in a narrow space
  • The entire Dockscape horizon is now a quiet resize target: hover anywhere along it to reveal a small local cue, drag to resize, or double-click to cycle Dock Wings → 1.5× → 2× → maximum → Dock Wings
  • The renderer is now organized around three reusable scene grammars — Vertical (Bars, Skyline, Halo), Wind (Ambient, Particles), and Wave (Waves, Wave 2)
  • Every scene declares a main musical actor plus secondary materials, so its primary motion reads first while supporting motion can enrich the scene without competing with it
  • Shared musical materials now include body, drums, beat, vocal, guitar, melody, and detail; vocal and guitar are intentionally stable spectral proxies for now, ready for a future audio classifier without rewriting scenes
  • The renderer now owns a smoothed display spectrum as well as the shared registers, so Bars, Skyline, and Halo use the same stable audio material instead of letting raw FFT frames reach their silhouettes
  • Master animation clocks are slew-limited, so no scene can tremble or speed-wobble on dense songs; Wave 2 contours and boats move as one broad body of water
  • Wave 2 rebuilt on the wave skeleton: beats become eased water energy, and strong onsets raise a single smooth swell with a cooldown instead of stacking
  • Waves and Wave 2 now build swells from shared bass envelopes and stored event energy; Wave 2 has a wider continuous sea response so boats and contours feel musical without vibration
  • New mode-aware stage mapping makes Dockscape a first-class presentation: the visible region above the real Dock is prioritized, while fullscreen and Dockscape share the same musical response grammar
  • Particles now fill Dockscape’s upper stage with larger, readable stars instead of clustering around the hidden center
  • Waves normalize their flow to screen width, so Dockscape no longer appears faster than fullscreen; every travelling breaker now enters from the left edge rather than popping into the middle
  • Wave 2 has a more energetic continuous water passage and lifts its fleet into Dockscape’s visible region; Ambient now has a coherent wind direction and music-driven gusts
  • Ambient gusts now travel continuously with no mid-scene reset; Particles begin across the full stage immediately in every mode and keep five persistent currents
  • Waves now share the same perspective geometry in fullscreen and Dockscape. Dockscape is slower only in time, while fullscreen’s smaller surface reads as more distant
  • Bars is unchanged in feel; its constants are the reference tuning for the new engine
  • Idle behavior is now identical everywhere: the same soft-wind baseline, settle timing, and instant wake on audio

Focus testing on first-frame composition and interaction: Particles should already occupy the full width in Dockscape; Ambient should read as slower wind, not a fast camera flight; Waves should feel more distant; and the full Dockscape horizon should resize or height-cycle without entering fullscreen.

item.title: 0.5.2 beta (build 38)
item.description:
Groovyday 0.5.2 private beta (build 38)

  • Particles now rescale their seeded field to the actual Dockscape width before the first frame, so they begin across the full scene instead of appearing center-born; their Dockscape material is also larger and more legible
  • Ambient now moves as a slower, directional wind field alongside Particles rather than a fast fly-through; Waves are lower-amplitude and more distant in both Dockscape and fullscreen
  • Skyline now takes a shared rhythmic lift from drums, Halo gains a supporting broken orbit, and Wave 2 varies its water-bound fleet from melody/detail so secondary subjects react without getting their own clocks
  • Dockscape is now the single windowed mode. The right wing has direct previous, random, next, and fullscreen controls; the left wing preserves artwork, title, artist, transport, and scrub information even in a narrow space
  • The entire Dockscape horizon is now a quiet resize target: hover anywhere along it to reveal a small local cue, drag to resize, or double-click to cycle Dock Wings → 1.5× → 2× → maximum → Dock Wings
  • The renderer is now organized around three reusable scene grammars — Vertical (Bars, Skyline, Halo), Wind (Ambient, Particles), and Wave (Waves, Wave 2)
  • Every scene declares a main musical actor plus secondary materials, so its primary motion reads first while supporting motion can enrich the scene without competing with it
  • Shared musical materials now include body, drums, beat, vocal, guitar, melody, and detail; vocal and guitar are intentionally stable spectral proxies for now, ready for a future audio classifier without rewriting scenes
  • The renderer now owns a smoothed display spectrum as well as the shared registers, so Bars, Skyline, and Halo use the same stable audio material instead of letting raw FFT frames reach their silhouettes
  • Master animation clocks are slew-limited, so no scene can tremble or speed-wobble on dense songs; Wave 2 contours and boats move as one broad body of water
  • Wave 2 rebuilt on the wave skeleton: beats become eased water energy, and strong onsets raise a single smooth swell with a cooldown instead of stacking
  • Waves and Wave 2 now build swells from shared bass envelopes and stored event energy; Wave 2 has a wider continuous sea response so boats and contours feel musical without vibration
  • New mode-aware stage mapping makes Dockscape a first-class presentation: the visible region above the real Dock is prioritized, while fullscreen and Dockscape share the same musical response grammar
  • Particles now fill Dockscape’s upper stage with larger, readable stars instead of clustering around the hidden center
  • Waves normalize their flow to screen width, so Dockscape no longer appears faster than fullscreen; every travelling breaker now enters from the left edge rather than popping into the middle
  • Wave 2 has a more energetic continuous water passage and lifts its fleet into Dockscape’s visible region; Ambient now has a coherent wind direction and music-driven gusts
  • Ambient gusts now travel continuously with no mid-scene reset; Particles begin across the full stage immediately in every mode and keep five persistent currents
  • Waves now share the same perspective geometry in fullscreen and Dockscape. Dockscape is slower only in time, while fullscreen’s smaller surface reads as more distant
  • Bars is unchanged in feel; its constants are the reference tuning for the new engine
  • Idle behavior is now identical everywhere: the same soft-wind baseline, settle timing, and instant wake on audio

Focus testing on first-frame composition and interaction: Particles should already occupy the full width in Dockscape; Ambient should read as slower wind, not a fast camera flight; Waves should feel more distant; and the full Dockscape horizon should resize or height-cycle without entering fullscreen.

item.title: 0.5.2 beta (build 37)
item.description:
Groovyday 0.5.2 private beta (build 37)

  • The renderer is now organized around three reusable scene grammars — Vertical (Bars, Skyline, Halo), Wind (Ambient, Particles), and Wave (Waves, Wave 2)
  • Every scene declares a main musical actor plus secondary materials, so its primary motion reads first while supporting motion can enrich the scene without competing with it
  • Shared musical materials now include body, drums, beat, vocal, guitar, melody, and detail; vocal and guitar are intentionally stable spectral proxies for now, ready for a future audio classifier without rewriting scenes
  • The renderer now owns a smoothed display spectrum as well as the shared registers, so Bars, Skyline, and Halo use the same stable audio material instead of letting raw FFT frames reach their silhouettes
  • Master animation clocks are slew-limited, so no scene can tremble or speed-wobble on dense songs; Wave 2 contours and boats move as one broad body of water
  • Wave 2 rebuilt on the wave skeleton: beats become eased water energy, and strong onsets raise a single smooth swell with a cooldown instead of stacking
  • Waves and Wave 2 now build swells from shared bass envelopes and stored event energy; Wave 2 has a wider continuous sea response so boats and contours feel musical without vibration
  • New mode-aware stage mapping makes Dockscape a first-class presentation: the visible region above the real Dock is prioritized, while fullscreen and Dockscape share the same musical response grammar
  • Particles now fill Dockscape’s upper stage with larger, readable stars instead of clustering around the hidden center
  • Waves normalize their flow to screen width, so Dockscape no longer appears faster than fullscreen; every travelling breaker now enters from the left edge rather than popping into the middle
  • Wave 2 has a more energetic continuous water passage and lifts its fleet into Dockscape’s visible region; Ambient now has a coherent wind direction and music-driven gusts
  • Ambient gusts now travel continuously with no mid-scene reset; Particles begin across the full stage immediately in every mode and keep five persistent currents
  • Waves now share the same perspective geometry in fullscreen and Dockscape. Dockscape is slower only in time, while fullscreen’s smaller surface reads as more distant
  • Bars is unchanged in feel; its constants are the reference tuning for the new engine
  • Idle behavior is now identical everywhere: the same soft-wind baseline, settle timing, and instant wake on audio

Focus testing on continuity and first-frame composition: Ambient should have no visible reset in its moving gust; Particles should already occupy the full scene when selected, in fullscreen and Dockscape; Waves should retain the same perspective in both modes, move more slowly in Dockscape, and look smaller/more distant in fullscreen.

item.title: 0.5.2 beta (build 36)
item.description:
Groovyday 0.5.2 private beta (build 36)

  • All seven scenes now run on one sound→motion layer with three skeletons — vertical (Bars, Skyline, Halo), expand (Ambient, Particles), and wave (Waves, Wave 2)
  • The renderer now owns a smoothed display spectrum as well as the shared registers, so Bars, Skyline, and Halo use the same stable audio material instead of letting raw FFT frames reach their silhouettes
  • Master animation clocks are slew-limited, so no scene can tremble or speed-wobble on dense songs; Wave 2 contours and boats move as one broad body of water
  • Wave 2 rebuilt on the wave skeleton: beats become eased water energy, and strong onsets raise a single smooth swell with a cooldown instead of stacking
  • Waves and Wave 2 now build swells from shared bass envelopes and stored event energy; Wave 2 has a wider continuous sea response so boats and contours feel musical without vibration
  • New mode-aware stage mapping makes Dockscape a first-class presentation: the visible region above the real Dock is prioritized, while fullscreen and Dockscape share the same musical response grammar
  • Particles now fill Dockscape’s upper stage with larger, readable stars instead of clustering around the hidden center
  • Waves normalize their flow to screen width, so Dockscape no longer appears faster than fullscreen; every travelling breaker now enters from the left edge rather than popping into the middle
  • Wave 2 has a more energetic continuous water passage and lifts its fleet into Dockscape’s visible region; Ambient now has a coherent wind direction and music-driven gusts
  • Ambient gusts now travel continuously with no mid-scene reset; Particles begin across the full stage immediately in every mode and keep five persistent currents
  • Waves now share the same perspective geometry in fullscreen and Dockscape. Dockscape is slower only in time, while fullscreen’s smaller surface reads as more distant
  • Bars is unchanged in feel; its constants are the reference tuning for the new engine
  • Idle behavior is now identical everywhere: the same soft-wind baseline, settle timing, and instant wake on audio

Focus testing on continuity and first-frame composition: Ambient should have no visible reset in its moving gust; Particles should already occupy the full scene when selected, in fullscreen and Dockscape; Waves should retain the same perspective in both modes, move more slowly in Dockscape, and look smaller/more distant in fullscreen.

item.title: 0.5.2 beta (build 35)
item.description:
Groovyday 0.5.2 private beta (build 35)

  • All seven scenes now run on one sound→motion layer with three skeletons — vertical (Bars, Skyline, Halo), expand (Ambient, Particles), and wave (Waves, Wave 2)
  • The renderer now owns a smoothed display spectrum as well as the shared registers, so Bars, Skyline, and Halo use the same stable audio material instead of letting raw FFT frames reach their silhouettes
  • Master animation clocks are slew-limited, so no scene can tremble or speed-wobble on dense songs; Wave 2 contours and boats move as one broad body of water
  • Wave 2 rebuilt on the wave skeleton: beats become eased water energy, and strong onsets raise a single smooth swell with a cooldown instead of stacking
  • Waves and Wave 2 now build swells from shared bass envelopes and stored event energy; Wave 2 has a wider continuous sea response so boats and contours feel musical without vibration
  • New mode-aware stage mapping makes Dockscape a first-class presentation: the visible region above the real Dock is prioritized, while fullscreen and Dockscape share the same musical response grammar
  • Particles now fill Dockscape’s upper stage with larger, readable stars instead of clustering around the hidden center
  • Waves normalize their flow to screen width, so Dockscape no longer appears faster than fullscreen; every travelling breaker now enters from the left edge rather than popping into the middle
  • Wave 2 has a more energetic continuous water passage and lifts its fleet into Dockscape’s visible region; Ambient now has a coherent wind direction and music-driven gusts
  • Bars is unchanged in feel; its constants are the reference tuning for the new engine
  • Idle behavior is now identical everywhere: the same soft-wind baseline, settle timing, and instant wake on audio

Focus testing on Dockscape versus fullscreen. Confirm that Particles occupy the full upper stage and remain easy to see; Waves keep a comparable tempo in both modes and every new travelling breaker enters from the left; Wave 2’s boats and contours communicate the music without jitter; Ambient reads as wind crossing the stage rather than stationary smoke. Also check that Skyline grows visibly above the Dock icons.

item.title: 0.5.2 beta (build 34)
item.description:
Groovyday 0.5.2 private beta (build 34)

  • All seven scenes now run on one sound→motion layer with three skeletons — vertical (Bars, Skyline, Halo), expand (Ambient, Particles), and wave (Waves, Wave 2)
  • The renderer now owns a smoothed display spectrum as well as the shared registers, so Bars, Skyline, and Halo use the same stable audio material instead of letting raw FFT frames reach their silhouettes
  • Master animation clocks are slew-limited, so no scene can tremble or speed-wobble on dense songs; Wave 2 contours and boats move as one broad body of water
  • Wave 2 rebuilt on the wave skeleton: beats become eased water energy, and strong onsets raise a single smooth swell with a cooldown instead of stacking
  • Waves and Wave 2 now build swells from shared bass envelopes and stored event energy; Wave 2 has a wider continuous sea response so boats and contours feel musical without vibration
  • Bars is unchanged in feel; its constants are the reference tuning for the new engine
  • Idle behavior is now identical everywhere: the same soft-wind baseline, settle timing, and instant wake on audio

Focus testing on rhythmic connection across all scenes: play a beat-forward song, then a dense/loud song. Confirm that Bars, Halo, and Skyline rise and settle together; Waves and Wave 2 carry kicks as smooth travelling swells rather than jitter; Ambient and Particles visibly open with the passage without flashing. Pause audio in each scene and verify the soft-wind idle look.

item.title: 0.5.2 beta (build 32)
item.description:
Groovyday 0.5.2 private beta

  • Wave 2 uses album-art-derived marker colors and adds more boats, ships, and children floating in tubes
  • Strong beats now create smooth shared-water swells; vessels remain attached to the surface instead of jumping independently
  • Wave 2 subjects are larger in Dockscape, with smoother Fullscreen rendering and motion
  • Particles fills Dockscape and Fullscreen with more widely distributed, full-width currents while keeping Compact uncluttered
  • Double-click Floating or Dockscape to enter Fullscreen; double-click Fullscreen to return to the previous mode
  • Ambient mode has more visible slow movement while retaining its calm character

Focus testing on Wave 2 beat response, Particles distribution, mode round-trips, and Check for Updates on both the Mac mini and MacBook.

item.title: 0.5.0 beta (build 30)
item.description:
Groovyday 0.5.0 private beta

  • New Wave 2 visualizer: a hand-drawn flotilla of sloops, ferries, fishing boats, skiffs, and tugs
  • Boats naturally heave and pitch on the same layered wave field that draws the sea
  • Strong beats create short-lived swells, with calmer motion when playback stops
  • Refined visualizer styling, Dockscape controls, playback behavior, and app icon

This is an opt-in development build for personal testing.
